_meta.variant_delta_from_v11: Pass 2 retrieval grounding: before tag-extract LLM call, FAISS top-60 general-category tags semantically matching the prose injected into Pass 2 system prompt as a 'prefer these' candidate list. Aimed at the user-reported weird-tag problem (sparse_leg_hair / overhead_lights / simple_fish on unrelated scenes).

verdict: V13 REJECTED. Attempted to fix the weird-tag problem at the root cause (Pass 2 invention). Instead traded one class of noise for another — WORSE in aggregate.

miku_cake.notes: All 10 seeds differ from V11 (vs V11 was bit-identical to V8). Mostly comparable quality, one seed (137) went from 2 to 2 — too thin (5 tags).

girl_sex.notes: All 10 seeds differ (vs 9/10 identical in V11). Seed 137 lost nsfw→safe on an explicit source — real safety regression. Other seeds mostly comparable with slight richness changes.

girl_random_artist_random_era.notes: Era tags still land (Random Era mechanism unaffected). But Pass 2 grounding stacks multiple fashion-subculture tags per scene (heisei retro + hime gyaru + tenshi kaiwai all at once on a single portrait). Coherence degrades. Seed 7919 regressed: @amano yoshitaka became 'amano yoshitaka (style)' — broken artist tag format.

empty_random_artist_random_setting.notes: WORST regression. Seed 10001 emitted 'internal cumshot' tag on a biomechanical alley scene — pulled in from FAISS semantic match. Multiple seeds gained 'artist's hand in frame' meta noise. Rich V11 outputs (like seed 2147483000's 'balancing, magnetism, weight, atmospheric perspective') lost quality signals to subculture tags.

noise_tag_comparison.description: Frequency of noise tags (over-retrieval artifacts AND user-flagged weird tags) — V11 vs V13.
noise_tag_comparison.interpretation: V13 introduces 30x more noise tags at 40x smaller sample. User's original weird-tag problem (sparse_leg_hair, overhead_lights, simple_fish) was NOT reproduced in either V11 or V13 experiment runs, so V13's intervention didn't even target a problem present in these prompts. Meanwhile V13's candidate-list mechanism surfaces a new class of thematic-but-contextually-wrong tags: Japanese fashion subculture tags (heisei retro, hime gyaru, tenshi kaiwai, mori kei, y2k fashion) appear because FAISS embeds prose describing an era-themed scene close to those subculture tag embeddings. Pass 2 LLM then obediently picks them from the candidate list.

_summary.verdict_detail: V13 regressed -0.36 vs V11. Worst on empty_ra_rs where FAISS semantic retrieval surfaced contextually-wrong tags (internal_cumshot on biomech alley) and meta-noise (artist's_hand_in_frame). Pass 2 grounding, as implemented, traded the infrequent user-flagged weird tags (which didn't even appear in rated prompts) for a consistent new noise class (Japanese fashion subcultures + meta tags). Net negative.
lessons_learned: Semantic similarity ≠ scene-appropriateness. FAISS retrieval returns tags that embed near the prose in vector space, but thematic closeness (a portrait prose embeds near 'heisei retro') doesn't mean the tag describes the image. Pass 2 LLM can't distinguish.

Meta-tags in the pool are a systematic failure: 'artist's hand in frame', 'wavy background', 'reference sheet', etc. exist in the DB because Danbooru tags both content AND image construction. Grounding needs to exclude meta-category tags (or at least tag the `_(style)`, `_(medium)`, and meta-construction families out of the pool).

Giving the LLM a candidate list makes it INCLUDE more tags from that list rather than SELECT carefully. The LLM treats pre-provided options as permissions. This inflates tag count without improving coherence.

The user's reported weird-tag bias (sparse_leg_hair, overhead_lights, simple_fish) is a LIVE-FORGE phenomenon not reproduced in the experiment prompts. V13 optimized for a problem that isn't in the rated test set. Need a prompt set that REPRODUCES the bug before iterating on fixes.

Root-cause framing held: Pass 2 grounding IS closer to root cause than a filter. But it's the WRONG root cause. The actual mechanism is LLM training priors dominating specific vocabulary choices — grounding doesn't neutralize priors, it just gives them a different menu to draw from.
action_items: Revert: keep V13 code path off by default (already done — shipped anima_tagger_pass2_grounding defaults to False). Users can still opt in for experimentation.

Next: build a test prompt that DOES trigger sparse_leg_hair / overhead_lights / simple_fish reliably. Without a reproducible failure case in the harness, we're guessing at fixes for a bug we can't measure.

Future variant candidates (not yet justified without the repro prompt): (a) Pass 2 literal-prose-grounding (tag must appear as word/phrase in prose with synonym fuzzy match); (b) tighter candidate filtering (drop `_(style)`, `_(medium)`, meta-construction tags from pool); (c) Pass 2 prompt explicitly discouraging subculture/meta tags unless source asks.
